 18995→ 13. Robot Motion & Vision Job Function
 18996→
 18997→      For robot motion functions and vision job functions to be available if using flow projects, it
 18998→requires the External Script to have the project flow enter the Listen node (external script control
 18999→mode) or the script node. If using script project programming, they are available in the project
 19000→programming directly.
 19001→
 19002→      Calling in the main thread, namely, the main flows or subflows in the flow projects, or the main
 19003→functions and the functions to call in the script projects, is mandatory for robot motion functions and
 19004→vision job functions moving to the initial position.Calling motion functions is not allowed in other
 19005→threads. All the motion processes will be queued temporarily and processed in sequence. Users
 19006→can use the queue tag numbers to understand the current motion command process if necessary.
 19007→
 19008→13.1 QueueTag()
 19009→
 19010→Set robot motions with Queue Tag Numbers to denote the current robot motion in process. The
 19011→status of each queue tag can be monitored using TMSTA SubCmd 01.

 19182→  Motion Function Queue Tag
 19183→
 19184→Motion function queue tags are used to cooperate with the robot motion functions. Since all
 19185→motion functions are queued in the buffer and executed in order, use the cooperative queue
 19186→tags, it is possible to know which motion function is in execution currently.
 19187→
 19188→1. < $TMSCT,172,2,float[] targetP1= {0,0,90,0,90,0}\r\n
 19189→
 19190→PTP("JPP",targetP1,10,200,0,false)\r\n
 19191→
 19192→QueueTag(1)\r\n                  // QueueTag(1) not wait and continue processing
 19193→
 19194→float[] targetP2 = {0,90,0,90,0,0}\r\n
 19195→
 19196→PTP("JPP",targetP2,10,200,10,false)\r\n
 19197→
 19198→QueueTag(2)\r\n                  // QueueTag(2) not wait and continue processing
 19199→
 19200→,*49\r\n
 19201→
 19202→When executed the script content, since QueueTag() did not wait, after execution, the
 19203→
 19204→process returned
 19205→
 19206→> $TMSCT,4,2,OK,*5F\r\n
 19207→
 19208→When robot motion executed PTP() targetP1, because of QueueTag(1), it will return
 19209→
 19210→> $TMSTA,10,01,01,true,*64\r\n          // TMSTA SubCmd 01, TagNumber 01, completed
 19211→
 19212→When robot motion executed PTP() targetP2, because of QueueTag(2) , it will return
 19213→
 19214→> $TMSTA,10,01,02,true,*67\r\n          // TMSTA SubCmd 01, TagNumber 02, completed
 19215→
 19216→2. < $TMSCT,174,2,float[] targetP1= {0,0,90,0,90,0}\r\n
 19217→
 19218→PTP("JPP",targetP1,10,200,0,false)\r\n
 19219→
 19220→QueueTag(3,1)\r\n // QueueTag(3) wait and stay in the function until the tagging completed
 19221→
 19222→float[] targetP2 = {0,90,0,90,0,0}\r\n
 19223→
 19224→PTP("JPP",targetP2,10,200,10,false)\r\n
 19225→
 19226→QueueTag(4)\r\n                  // QueueTag(4) not wait and continue processing
 19227→
 19228→,*56\r\n
 19229→
 19230→When executed the script content, since QueueTag(3,1) is set to wait, after tagging
 19231→
 19232→completed, the process returned
 19233→
 19234→> $TMSTA,10,01,03,true,*66\r\n          // TMSTA SubCmd 01, TagNumber 03, completed
 19235→
 19236→When QueueTag(3) completed, the process continues, since QueueTag(4) is not set to wait,
 19237→
 19238→after execution, the process returned
 19239→
 19240→> $TMSCT,4,2,OK,*5F\r\n
 19241→
 19242→When robot motion executed PTP() targetP2, because of QueueTag(4) , it will return
 19243→
 19244→> $TMSTA,10,01,04,true,*61\r\n          // TMSTA SubCmd 01, TagNumber 04, completed
 19245→
 19246→* $TMSCT,4,2,OK is returned when the process executed the script. Therefore, if using
 19247→QueueTag to wait or WaitQueueTag to wait, it will return after the execution as well.

 20940→ 13.15 CollisionCheck()
 20941→
 20942→      Pre-collision checks help prevent robots from interfering with self-testing and avoid collisions between
 20943→the gripper and the box. By adjusting and modifying the exported scene from TMstudio or using the built-in
 20944→box container in TMvision, users can simulate robot motions using robot parts, tools, and virtual boxes.
 20945→This simulation allows for early detection of potential issues during motions, reducing collisions in
 20946→applications like box picking, using large hardware, and executing critical poses.
 20947→
 20948→Syntax 1
 20949→
 20950→      string CollisionCheck(
 20951→             float,
 20952→             string
 20953→
 20954→      )
 20955→
 20956→      Parameter
 20957→             float Safety distance (mm)
 20958→             string The subflow name. For use in flow projects only. Returns errors if using in script
 20959→                          projects.
 20960→
 20961→      Return
 20962→             string the result of checking
 20963→                                == "" The value returned is an empty string, and there was no collision
 20964→                                              detected.
 20965→                                != "" The value returned is a non-empty string, and there was a collision
 20966→                                              detected. The contest will indicate which node.
 20967→

20994→                        detected. The contest will indicate which line number.
 20995→
 20996→      Only motion functions checks PTP(), Line(), Circle(), Move_PTP(), and Move_Line() only.
 20997→
 20998→      Only supports variable operations, general functions, math functions, and parameterized objects
 20999→      (read only). Does not operate with other functions.
 21000→
 21001→      The result of the variable operation is vaild only during the pre-checking. The values revert to the
 21002→      default after checking.
 21003→
 21004→      All statements within the conditional expression will be checked.
 21005→
 21006→      All statements within the loop expression will be checked only once and will not repeat.
 21007→Note
 21008→
 21009→   (1)
 21010→      string result = CollisionCheck(15, "CCheck0")
 21011→

 21036→
 21037→main
 21038→
 21039→{
 21040→
 21041→      Display(count)                // count = 0
 21042→
 21043→      Pause()
 21044→
 21045→      string re = CollisionCheck(15, TargetMotion())
 21046→
 21047→      // Set safety distance to 15 mm for TargetMotion() function to check
 21048→
 21049→      if (re == "") TargetMotion() // No collision detected. Call TargetMotion() for the real operation.
 21050→
 21051→      else Display("Collision: " + re) // Collistion detected.
 21052→
 21053→      Display(count)                // count = 0 or 1
 21054→
 21055→      // +1 during pre-checking. Revert to the default, 0, after checking. If no collision detected, it calls the function
 21056→
 21057→      TargetMotion(), and the the count value is 1. If collision detected, it does not call the function TargetMotion(), and
 21058→
 21059→      the the count value is 0.
 21060→
 21061→}
 21062→
 21063→void TargetMotion()
 21064→
 21065→{
 21066→
 21067→      count++                       // count = 1 // Support variable operation during pre-checking.
 21068→
 21069→      while (true)                  // The loop expression will be checked once only during pre-checking.
 21070→
 21071→      {
 21072→
 21073→                    PTP("CPP",Point["P2"].Value,50,200,100,false)                      // check
 21074→
 21075→                    // check only during pre-checking without motion
 21076→
 21077→                    PTP("JPP",Point["P3"].Joint,50,200,100,false)                      // check
 21078→
 21079→                    Move_PTP("JPP",{0,0,0,0,0,60},50,200,100,false) // check
 21080→
 21081→                    Line("CPP",Point["P4"].Value,50,200,100,false)                     // check
 21082→
 21083→                    Move_Line("CPP", {20,20,20,0,0,0},50,200,100,false) // check
 21084→
 21085→                    Circle("CPP", Point["P2"].Value, Point["P3"].Value, 50, 200, 100, 100, false) // check
 21086→
 21087→                    WaitQueueTag()  // ignore // Do not support to this function during pre-checking.
 21088→
 21089→                    byte[] bb = modbus_read("localhost", 1, "DO", 0, 10)
 21090→
 21091→                    // ignore // Do not support to this function during pre-checking.
 21092→
 21093→                    string ss = GetString(bb) // General function. The operation result is ss = "{}".
 21094→
 21095→      }
 21096→
 21097→}

 22245→ 14.5 Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue()
 22246→
 22247→Retrieve the output variable values after the vision IO Trigger Job execution.. (Retrieve and
 22248→remove from the buffer.)
 22249→
 22250→Syntax 1
 22251→
 22252→string[] Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue(
 22253→
 22254→   string
 22255→
 22256→)
 22257→
 22258→Parameter
 22259→
 22260→   string           The variable name as output by the IO Trigger Job with the data type of
 22261→
 22262→                    string array.
 22263→
 22264→Return
 22265→
 22266→   string[]         The output values of the designated variables in the buffer.
 22267→
 22268→Note
 22269→      After the vision IO Trigger Job execution (Enabling the IO trigger mode is a must during
 22270→
 22271→vision editing.), the result updates to the variable after each IO trigger. Since the output of the
 22272→IO Trigger Job is multiple outputs, to prevent the output data from being overwritten because
 22273→of out-of-process, it stores the output data in the buffer one by one (first in, first out), and
 22274→users can use the function calls to retrieve the count or the values in the buffer. For example,
 22275→job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM is an output variable of the vision job job1 to be
 22276→output by the Counting (Blobs) module 1. After the vision execution, it updates and stores the
 22277→result in the buffer at each IO trigger, so users can call the functions to retrieve the count or
 22278→the values of the variable job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM in the buffer.
 22279→
 22280→      The buffer comes with a capacity limit. When results are about to enter, if the buffer
 22281→capacity is insufficient, it removes the oldest data automatically and adds the latest to the
 22282→buffer.
 22283→
 22284→      Suppose the current content in the buffer goes by { {"0", "1", "2"}, {"3", "4"}, {"5"} }
 22285→      Retrieve the output variable count of the vision trigger job in the buffer.
 22286→      int var_count = Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputCount
 22287→("job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM")
 22288→
 22289→          // var_count = 3
 22290→
 22291→      Retrieve the output variable values in the buffer.
 22292→      string[] var_s = Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue
 22293→("job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM")
 22294→
 22295→          // var_s = {"0", "1", "2"}
 22296→
 22297→          // After retrieving, the content in the buffer turns to { {"3","4"}, {"5"} }.
 22298→
 22299→      var_count = Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputCount
 22300→("job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM")
 22301→
 22302→          // var_count = 2
 22303→
 22304→      var_s = Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue ("job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM")
 22305→
 22306→          // var_s = {"3","4"}
 22307→
 22308→          // After retrieving, the content in the buffer turns to { {"5"} }.
 22309→
 22310→      var_s = Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue ("job1_Count_Blob_1_DetectObjectX_TM")
 22311→
 22312→          // var_s = {"5"}

 22335→ 15. External Script
 22336→
 22337→15.1 Listen Node
 22338→
 22339→Users can establish a socket TCPlistener (server site) in the listen node to connect to external
 22340→devices and communicate based on the packet format. All features available in
 22341→TM_Robot_Function can also be operated in the listen node.
 22342→
 22343→                                                 1. Send Message: When entering this node, it will initiate
 22344→                                                       a message
 22345→
 22346→                                                 2. Print Log: Enable Communication Log (shown on the
 22347→                                                       right)
 22348→
 22349→                                                 3. Connection Timeout: When entering this node, if
 22350→                                                       more than the time (milliseconds) is not connected, it
 22351→                                                       will be overtime.
 22352→                                                       If <= 0, no timeout
 22353→
 22354→                                                 4. Data Timeout: When connected, the timeout will be
 22355→                                                       exceeded when there is no communication packet
 22356→                                                       If <= 0, no timeout
 22357→
 22358→Socket TCPListener is started up after the project being executed, and closed as the project
 22359→
 22360→stopped. The IP and listen port will be shown on the Notice Log window on the right, after the
 22361→
 22362→Socket TCPListener is started up.
 22363→
 22364→IP  HMI  System  Network  IP Address
 22365→
 22366→Port 5890
 22367→
 22368→When entering the Listen Node, the flow will keep at Listen Node until either of the two exit
 22369→
 22370→conditions is fulfilled.
 22371→
 22372→Pass: ScriptExit() is executed or the project is stopped
 22373→
 22374→Fail: 1. Connection Timeout
 22375→
 22376→    2. Data Timeout
 22377→
 22378→    3. Before the TCP Listener is started up, the flow has entered this Listen Node
 22379→
 22380→The command received by listen node will be executed in order. If the command is not valid, an
 22381→error message will be returned carrying the line number with errors. If the command is valid, it will
 22382→be executed.
 22383→
 22384→The command can be divided into two categories. The first category is commands which can be
 22385→accomplished in instance, like assigning variable value. The second category is commands needs
 22386→to be executed in sequence, like motion command and IO value assigning. The second category
 22387→command will be placed in queue and executed in order.
 22388→

 22394→ 15.2 ScriptListen()
 22395→
 22396→Enter external script control mode.
 22397→
 22398→Syntax 1
 22399→
 22400→bool ScriptListen(
 22401→      string,
 22402→      bool,
 22403→      int,
 22404→      int
 22405→
 22406→)
 22407→
 22408→Parameters
 22409→
 22410→string The message string to send while in the external script control mode.
 22411→
 22412→bool Whether to display the receiving external messages in the notice window.
 22413→
 22414→int                 After entering external Script control mode, it will time out if not connected within a
 22415→
 22416→                    specific timeframe (measured in milliseconds).
 22417→
 22418→                    If <= 0 No timeout
 22419→
 22420→Int                 After entering external Script control mode and connected, it will time out if no
 22421→
 22422→                    communication packets received within a specific timeframe (measured in
 22423→
 22424→                    milliseconds).
 22425→
 22426→                    If <= 0 No timeout
 22427→
 22428→Return              When entering the external Script control mode, it will remain there until meeting
 22429→      bool          certain conditions and exit by the conditions.
 22430→
 22431→                          True execute ScriptExit() or stop the project
 22432→
 22433→                    False 1. Connection Timeout occured
 22434→
 22435→                                    2. Data Timeout occured
 22436→
 22437→                                    3. If the TCP Listener fails to set up, ScriptListen() is invoked.
 22438→
 22439→            Accessible only through the Listen node, the Script node, or Script project
 22440→
 22441→            programming.
 22442→
 22443→            Calling in the flow required.

 22487→ 15.3 ScriptExit()
 22488→
 22489→Exit external script control mode.
 22490→
 22491→Syntax 1
 22492→      bool ScriptExit(
 22493→      )

22493→      )
 22494→      Parameters
 22495→            void No parameter
 22496→      Return
 22497→            bool True Command accepted; False Command rejected (format error)
 22498→      Note
 22499→            Exit the external script control mode and wait for the command to finish, and then quit the
 22500→            listen node and move on with the pass route, or quit ScriptListen() and return True.
 22501→
 22502→* Execute via TMSCT communication packets required.
 22503→* Functions after ScriptExit() will not be executed such as
 22504→
 22505→< $TMSCT,86,2,ChangeBase("RobotBase")\r\n
 22506→
 22507→                    ChangeTCP("RobotEndFlange")\r\n
 22508→
 22509→                    ScriptExit()\r\n                  // Exit the external script control mode.
 22510→
 22511→                    ChangeLoad(10.1),*58\r\n          // ChangeLoad will not be executed.
 22512→
 22513→* After exiting the script mode, it is required to wait for all the commands and the
 22514→functions to complete executions until quitting the listen node and moving on with the
 22515→pass route. At the time being of waiting for quitting the listen node, it is not in the external
 22516→script control mode, so no more external commands will be accepted and CPEER error
 22517→packets will be replied.
 22518→
 22519→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                        Document Version: 1.0  366
 22520→

 22524→ 15.4 Communication Protocol
 22525→
 22526→                                                           Length
 22527→
 22528→Start Byte  Hdr            Len            Data                                    Checksum End Byte1 End Byte2
 22529→
 22530→$           Header  ,      Length      ,  Data                              ,  *  Checksum            \r  \n
 22531→
 22532→                           Checksum (XOR of these Bytes)
 22533→
 22534→Name        Size ASCII HEX                Description
 22535→Start Byte                                Start Byte for Communication
 22536→Header      1          $   0x24           Header for Communication
 22537→Separator                                 Separator between Header and Length
 22538→Length      X                             Length of Data
 22539→Separator                                 Separator between Length and Data
 22540→Data        1          ,   0x2C           Communication Data
 22541→Separator                                 Separator between Data and Checksum
 22542→Sign        Y                             Begin Sign of Checksum
 22543→Checksum                                  Checksum of Communication
 22544→End Byte 1  1          ,   0x2C
 22545→End Byte 2                                End Byte of Communication
 22546→            Z
 22547→
 22548→            1          ,   0x2C
 22549→
 22550→            1          *   0x2A
 22551→
 22552→            2
 22553→
 22554→            1          \r  0x0D
 22555→
 22556→            1          \n  0x0A
 22557→
 22558→1. Header
 22559→    Defines the purpose of the communication package. The data definition could be different with
 22560→    different Header.
 22561→       TMSCT External Script
 22562→       TMSTA Acquiring status or properties
 22563→       CPERR Communication data error (E.g. Packet error, checksum error, header error,
 22564→            etc.)
 22565→
 22566→2. Length
 22567→
 22568→Length defines the length in UTF8 byte. It can be represented in decimal, hexadecimal or
 22569→
 22570→binary, the upper limit is int 32bits
 22571→
 22572→Example:
 22573→
 22574→   $TMSCT,100,Data,*CS\r\n // Decimal 100, that is the data length is 100 bytes
 22575→
 22576→   $TMSCT,0x100,Data,*CS\r\n // Hexadecimal 0x100, that is the data length is 256 bytes
 22577→
 22578→   $TMSCT,0b100,Data,*CS\r\n // Binary 0b100, that is the data length is 4 bytes
 22579→
 22580→   $TMSCT,8,1, ,*58\r\n                   // The Data length 1,                is 8 bytes (UTF8)
 22581→
 22582→3. Data
 22583→    The content of the communication package. Arbitrary characters are supported (including
 22584→    0x00 .. 0xFF in UTF8). The data length is defined in Length and the purpose is defined in
 22585→    Header
 22586→
 22587→4. Checksum
 22588→    The checksum of the communication package. The checksum is calculated with
 22589→    XOR(exclusive OR), and the range for checksum computation starts from $ to * ($ and * are
 22590→
 22591→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                           Document Version: 1.0      367
 22592→
 22593→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 22594→
 22595→5F., No. 58-2, Huaya 2nd Rd., Guishan Dist., Taoyuan City, 333411 , Taiwan
 22596→ excluded) as shown below:
 22597→             $TMSCT,100,Data,*CS\r\n
 22598→
 22599→Checksum = Byte[1] ^ Byte[2] ... ^ Byte[N-6]
 22600→The representation of checksum is fixed to 2 bytes in hexadecimal format (without 0x).
 22601→For example:
 22602→
 22603→             $TMSCT,5,10,OK,*6D
 22604→CS = 0x54 ^ 0x4D ^ 0x53 ^ 0x43 ^ 0x54 ^ 0x2C ^ 0x35 ^ 0x2C ^ 0x31 ^ 0x30 ^ 0x2C ^ 0x4F ^
 22605→0x4B ^ 0x2C = 0x6D
 22606→CS = 6D (0x36 0x44)
 22607→
 22608→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                        Document Version: 1.0  368
 22609→
 22610→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 22611→
 22612→5F., No. 58-2, Huaya 2nd Rd., Guishan Dist., Taoyuan City, 333411 , Taiwan
 22613→ 15.5 TMSCT
 22614→
 22615→Start Byte  Hdr                Len             Data                               Checksum End Byte1 End Byte2
 22616→                                               Data
 22617→$           TMSCT      ,       Length    ,                                  ,  *  Checksum            \r   \n
 22618→                                              ,
 22619→                              ID                                                    SCRIPT
 22620→                          Script ID                                            Script Language
 22621→
 22622→TMSCT defines the communication package as External Script Language. In External Script
 22623→
 22624→Language, the data contains two parts and is separated by comma. One is ID and the other is
 22625→
 22626→SCRIPT
 22627→
 22628→        ID          Script ID, can be arbitrary English character or number (a CPERR 04 error will
 22629→
 22630→                    be reported when encountering non-alphanumeric byte). The ID is used as
 22631→
 22632→                    specifying the target SCRIPT of return message.
 22633→
 22634→        ,           Separator
 22635→
 22636→        SCRIPT The content defined in Script Language. In a communication package, multi-line
 22637→
 22638→                    scripts can fit into the SCRIPT section with separator (0x0D 0x0A)
 22639→
 22640→Note
 22641→
 22642→        TMSCT is available only when in the external script control mode, otherwise CPEER
 22643→
 22644→        error packets will be replied.
 22645→
 22646→Return (RobotExternal Device)
 22647→
 22648→        1. When it enters Listen Node, the robot will send a message to all the connected
 22649→
 22650→            device. The ID is set to 0.
 22651→
 22652→            $TMSCT,9,0,Listen1,*4C\r\n
 22653→
 22654→                    9     The length of 0,Listen1 is 9 bytes
 22655→
 22656→                    0     The Script ID is 0
 22657→
 22658→                    ,     Separator
 22659→
 22660→                    Listen1 The message to send
 22661→
 22662→        2. The OK or ERROR message is replied according to the Script's content. For
 22663→
 22664→            message with ;N, ;N represents the number of line with error or warning. After the
 22665→
 22666→            message is received, robot will execute the message, then send back the return
 22667→
 22668→            message, if the Script is valid. For invalid Script, the return message will be sent back
 22669→
 22670→            immediately without executed.
 22671→
 22672→            $TMSCT,4,1,OK,*5C\r\n                     // Response to ID 1
 22673→
 22674→                                              // OK means valid Script.
 22675→
 22676→            $TMSCT,8,2,OK;2;3,*52\r\n                 // Response to ID 2
 22677→
 22678→                                              // OK;2;3 means valid Script with warnings in line 2 and 3.
 22679→
 22680→            $TMSCT,13,3,ERROR;1;2;3,*3F\r\n // Response to ID 3
 22681→
 22682→                                              // ERROR;1;2;3 means invalid Script with errors in line 1, 2 and
 22683→
 22684→                                              3.
 22685→
 22686→Receive (RobotExternal Device)
 22687→
 22688→        1. When it enters the listen node, the robot will start to receive, check, and execute the
 22689→
 22690→            external script. If the robot did not enter the listen node (not in the external script
 22691→
 22692→            control mode), the Script received will be disposed and CPEER error packets will be
 22693→
 22694→            replied.
 22695→
 22696→        2. The message from external device should define the Script ID as a ID used in
 22697→
 22698→            messages replied by robot.
 22699→
 22700→            < $TMSCT,25,1,ChangeBase("RobotBase"),*08\r\n // Defined as ID 1
 22701→
 22702→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                           Document Version: 1.0      369
 22703→
 22704→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 22705→
 22706→5F., No. 58-2, Huaya 2nd Rd., Guishan Dist., Taoyuan City, 333411 , Taiwan
 22707→ > $TMSCT,4,1,OK,*5C\r\n                                                     // Response to ID 1
 22708→
 22709→3. In a communication package, multi-line scripts can fit into the SCRIPT section with
 22710→    separator \r\n
 22711→
 22712→< $TMSCT, 72,2,ChangeBase("RobotBase")\r\n
 22713→      ChangeTCP("RobotEndFlange")\r\n
 22714→      ChangeLoad(10.1),* 5A\r\n // Three lines Script in a communication package (Lines
 22715→
 22716→       are separated by \r\n)
 22717→
 22718→> $TMSCT,4,2,OK,*5F\r\n
 22719→
 22720→4. In Listen Node, local variables are supported and valid before quitting the Listen
 22721→    Node.
 22722→
 22723→< $TMSCT,40,3,int var_i = 100\r\n
 22724→      var_i = 1000\r\n
 22725→      var_i++,*5A\r\n
 22726→
 22727→> $TMSCT,4,3,OK,*5E\r\n
 22728→
 22729→ < $TMSCT,42,4,int var_i = 100\r\n                                          // Because int var_i has been declared, an error
 22730→       var_i = 1000\r\n
 22731→       var_i++\r\n
 22732→       ,*58\r\n
 22733→
 22734→ > $TMSCT,9,4,ERROR;1,*02\r\n
 22735→
 22736→occurred.
 22737→
 22738→5. In the listen node, it is possible to access or modify the project's variables, but no new
 22739→    variable can be declared since the variables created in the listen node are local
 22740→    variables.
 22741→
 22742→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                        Document Version: 1.0  370
 22743→
 22744→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 22745→
 22746→5F., No. 58-2, Huaya 2nd Rd., Guishan Dist., Taoyuan City, 333411 , Taiwan
 22747→ 15.6 TMSTA
 22748→
 22749→Start Byte  Hdr               Len           Data                                     Checksum End Byte1 End Byte2
 22750→
 22751→$           TMSTA          ,  Length     ,  Data                            ,     *  Checksum         \r  \n
 22752→
 22753→                        SubCmd
 22754→
 22755→                        SubCmd              ...                                      ...              (Based on SubCmd)
 22756→
 22757→TMSTA defines the communication package as acquiring status or properties. The data section of
 22758→
 22759→the package contains different sub command (SubCmd). The package format could be different
 22760→
 22761→according to different SubCmd. The definitions are listed below.
 22762→
 22763→SubCmd
 22764→
 22765→   00               In external script control mode or not
 22766→
 22767→   01               Complete the configured QueueTag numbering or not
 22768→
 22769→   90..99 Date message to send (the format of data is self-definable)
 22770→
 22771→Note
 22772→
 22773→   TMSTA could be executed without entering the Listen Node
 22774→
 22775→SubCmd 00 In external script control mode or not
 22776→
 22777→Format
 22778→
 22779→   Response (RobotExternal Device)
 22780→
 22781→            SubCmd                          Entry                                         Message
 22782→                                                                                          message
 22783→                    00          ,           false                              ,
 22784→
 22785→                    00          ,           true                               ,
 22786→
 22787→   Receive (RobotExternal Device)
 22788→
 22789→            SubCmd
 22790→
 22791→                    00
 22792→
 22793→Response (RobotExternal Device)
 22794→
 22795→   1. If not in external script control mode, it will reply false.
 22796→
 22797→            $TMSTA,9,00,false,,*37\r\n
 22798→
 22799→                    9         Indicates the length of 00,false, is 9 bytes
 22800→
 22801→                    00        Indicates SubCmd as 00
 22802→
 22803→                    ,         Separator
 22804→
 22805→                    false       The flow has not entered Listen Node
 22806→
 22807→                    ,         Separator
 22808→
 22809→                              Empty string (Have not entered Listen Node)
 22810→
 22811→   2. If in external script control mode, it will reply true.
 22812→
 22813→            $TMSTA,15,00,true,Listen1,*79\r\n
 22814→
 22815→                    15        Indicates the length of 00,true,Listen1 is 15 bytes
 22816→
 22817→                    00        Indicates SubCmd as 00
 22818→
 22819→                    ,         Separator
 22820→
 22821→                    true The flow has entered the Listen Node
 22822→
 22823→                    ,         Separator
 22824→
 22825→                    Listen1 The message to be sent as in Listen Node (It indicates the flow is in
 22826→
 22827→            Listen1)
 22828→

 22834→ Receive (RobotExternal Device)
 22835→
 22836→1. Send to the robot from the external device
 22837→
 22838→        $TMSTA,2,00,*41\r\n
 22839→
 22840→                    2   Indicates the length of 00 is 2 bytes.
 22841→
 22842→                    00  Indicates the SubCmd is 00 whether in external script control mode or
 22843→
 22844→        not.
 22845→
 22846→SubCmd 01 Complete the configured QueueTag numbering or not
 22847→
 22848→Format
 22849→
 22850→Send (RobotExternal Device)
 22851→
 22852→        SubCmd                        Tag Number                               Status
 22853→
 22854→                    01  ,             01 .. 15                              ,  true/false/none
 22855→
 22856→Receive (RobotExternal Device)
 22857→
 22858→        SubCmd                        Tag Number
 22859→
 22860→                    01     ,             01 .. 15
 22861→
 22862→Note
 22863→
 22864→When inquiring with TMSTA 01, users can look up to the status of the last 4 tag numbers.
 22865→
 22866→Send (RobotExternal Device)
 22867→
 22868→1. Send to the external device from the robot. Spontaneously sending after QueueTag
 22869→
 22870→        numbering completed.
 22871→
 22872→        $TMSTA,10,01,08,true,*6D\r\n
 22873→
 22874→                    10  Indicates the length of 01,00,true is 10 bytes
 22875→
 22876→                    01  Indicates SubCmd as 01 to send the status of Tag Number
 22877→
 22878→                    ,   Separation symbol
 22879→
 22880→                    08  Tag Number 08
 22881→
 22882→                    ,   Separation symbol
 22883→
 22884→                    true true Indicates Tag Number complete
 22885→
 22886→                        false Indicates Tag Number incomplete
 22887→
 22888→                        none Indicates Tag Number not existed
 22889→
 22890→Receive (RobotExternal Device)
 22891→
 22892→1. Send from the external device to the robot. Users can look up to the status of the last
 22893→
 22894→        4 tag numbers.
 22895→
 22896→        $TMSTA,5,01,15,*6F\r\n
 22897→
 22898→                    5   Indicates the length of 01,88 is 5 bytes
 22899→
 22900→                    01  Indicates SubCmd as 01 to send the status of Tag Number
 22901→
 22902→                    ,   Separation symbol
 22903→
 22904→                    15  Tag Number 15
 22905→
 22906→        > $TMSTA,10,01,15,none,*7D\r\n                                      // TagNumber 15 not existed
 22907→
 22908→2. Tag Number uses the value of integers between 1 and 15. If the value is invalid, it
 22909→
 22910→        relies none for not existed.
 22911→
 22912→        $TMSTA,5,01,88,*6B\r\n
 22913→
 22914→        > $TMSTA,10,01,88,none,*79\r\n                                      // TagNumber 88 not existed

 22958→       1. Send to the external device from the robot. When the external script executes the
 22959→            ListenSend() function, it will send data.
 22960→            string var_s = "Hello World"

 22962→            byte[] var_b = {0x10, 0x11, 0x12, 0x13}

 22985→ 15.7 CPERR
 22986→
 22987→Start Byte  Hdr        Len                 Data                                   Checksum End Byte1 End Byte2
 22988→
 22989→$           CPERR   ,  Length           ,  Data                             ,  *  Checksum            \r  \n
 22990→
 22991→                                             Error Code
 22992→                                           Code (00 .. FF)
 22993→
 22994→CPERR defines the communication package as sending the Communication Protocol Error. The
 22995→
 22996→data section is defined as Error Code.
 22997→
 22998→Error Code          Error code, presented in 2 bytes hexadecimal format (without 0x)
 22999→
 23000→   00                  Packet correct. No error. (The return message usually reply to the content
 23001→
 23002→                       of packet instead of returning no error)
 23003→
 23004→   01               Packet Error.
 23005→
 23006→   02               Checksum Error.
 23007→
 23008→   03               Header Error.
 23009→
 23010→   04               Packet Data Error.
 23011→
 23012→   F1               Have not entered Listen Node
 23013→
 23014→Note
 23015→
 23016→   Used by robot to response to external device
 23017→

 23019→      01 Packet Error

 23032→
 23033→   01 Packet Error

 23038→
 23039→            of 26 exceeds the actual data amount, indicating an insufficient number of packets.
 23040→

 23042→

 23044→

 23122→ 15.8 Priority Commands
 23123→
 23124→    Due to the serial execution nature of the TMscript syntax, if using the queue syntax such as
 23125→QueueTag(1, 1) or WaitQueueTag(1) to wait for the tag number to arrive, the program will stay put
 23126→until the conditions are comprehended before going on execution. Therefore, if received the
 23127→ScriptExit() syntax sent from the outside while waiting, it is impossible to exit the external Script
 23128→control mode since the program is still waiting for the condition to meet.
 23129→
 23130→    When the program comes to the Listen node (the external Script control mode), other than the
 23131→serial execution TMscript syntax, there are also priority commands to use. In Listen nodes, the
 23132→priority commands go with a higher execution priority than the syntax execution, and the priority
 23133→commands will run at once as defined below.
 23134→
 23135→1. ScriptExit(0)
 23136→    Stop the robot motion immediately, clear the robot motion instructions in the buffer, and exit the
 23137→    external Script control mode. Go to the Fail path after leaving the Listen node.
 23138→
 23139→2. ScriptExit(1)
 23140→    Stop the robot motion immediately, clear the robot motion instructions in the buffer, and exit the
 23141→    external Script control mode. Go to the Pass path after leaving the Listen node.
 23142→
 23143→3. StopAndClearBuffer(0)
 23144→    Stop the robot motion immediately and clear the robot motion instructions in the buffer.
 23145→
 23146→4. StopAndClearBuffer(1)
 23147→    Stop the robot motion immediately, clear the robot motion instructions in the buffer, exit the
 23148→    current Script program in execution, and continue to the next Script program.
 23149→
 23150→5. StopAndClearBuffer(2)
 23151→    Stop the robot motion immediately, clear the robot motion instructions in the buffer, exit the
 23152→    current Script program in execution, and clear all the script programs in the Received Script
 23153→    buffer.
 23154→
 23155→ Priority commands support the general use of the command definitions only but not the use
 23156→
 23157→with variables and functions such as
 23158→
 23159→< $TMSCT,42,1,int var_st=2\r\n
 23160→
 23161→StopAndClearBuffer(var_st),*3A\r\n
 23162→
 23163→> $TMSCT,9,1,ERROR;2,*04\r\n          // Invalid syntax StopAndClearBuffer(var_st)
 23164→
 23165→ Using priority commands with TMscript syntax leads to priority command executions only
 23166→
 23167→but not syntax executions.
 23168→
 23169→< $TMSCT,94,2,float[] targetP1= {0,0,90,0,90,0}\r\n // Will not execute.
 23170→
 23171→PTP("JPP",targetP1,10,200,0,false)\r\n                                      // Will not execute.
 23172→
 23173→StopAndClearBuffer(0),*75\r\n                                               // Priority execution StopAndClearBuffer(0)
 23174→
 23175→> $TMSCT,4,2,OK,*5F\r\n
 23176→
 23177→ The system will handle one priority command only in one external Script packet. If the
 23178→    packet comes with numerous priority commands, the system will handle ScriptExit(0/1)
 23179→    before StopAndClearBuffer(0/1/2). If there are numerous ScriptExit and
 23180→
 23181→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                        Document Version: 1.0  376
 23182→
 23183→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 23184→

 23186→ StopAndClearBuffer respectively, the system will handle the first only.
 23187→
 23188→< $TMSCT,46,3,StopAndClearBuffer(2)\r\n // Will execute.
 23189→
 23190→                                     // There are many of StopAndClearBuffer and the system will
 23191→

 23200→StopAndClearBuffer(1)\r\n                             // Will not execute.

 23359→16.1 ModbusTCP Class
 23360→
 23361→Use Modbus TCP class and declare variables to create a Modbus TCP device. The variable
 23362→name will be the device name.
 23363→
 23364→Construct 1
 23365→
 23366→ModbusTCP VariableName = string, int, int
 23367→
 23368→ModbusTCP VariableName = string, int
 23369→
 23370→ModbusTCP VariableName = string
 23371→
 23372→Parameters
 23373→
 23374→   string remote host IP address
 23375→
 23376→   int              remote host connection port                             (502 by default)
 23377→
 23378→   int              read/write timeout in millisecond 0 .. 10000 (10000ms by default)
 23379→
 23380→Note
 23381→
 23382→   ModbusTCP mtcp1 = "192.168.1.10"
 23383→
 23384→          // construct a device, with IP 192.168.1.10
 23385→
 23386→   ModbusTCP mtcp2 = "192.168.1.10", 502
 23387→
 23388→          // construct a device, with IP 192.168.1.10, Port 502
 23389→
 23390→   ModbusTCP mtcp3 = "192.168.1.10", 502, 8000
 23391→
 23392→          // construct a device, with IP 192.168.1.10, Port 502, Timeout 8000ms
 23393→
 23394→   * After construction, either in flow projects or script projects, the device will not connect
 23395→   actively until proceeding to read or write.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation thoroughly to create a comprehensive summary.

The conversation is a continuation of a previous session where the user asked to read a TMscript programming language manual PDF from beginning to end. The goal is to extract a complete language reference.

From the previous summary, we know:
- User requested complete reading of TMscript 2.18 PDF
- File extracted to tmscript_full.txt (34,680 lines)
- Lines 1-12000 were read in the previous session
- We were at Section 7 File Functions, File_ReadLines() Syntax 3

In this session, I continued reading from offset 12000 to offset 23992 (where the summary was requested).

Sections covered in this session:
1. Section 7 (File Functions) - Lines 12000-14297: File_ReadLines (complete), File_NextLine (all 5 syntaxes), File_NextEOF, File_WriteBytes, File_WriteText, File_WriteLine, File_WriteLines, File_Exists, File_Length, File_Delete, File_Copy, File_CopyImage, File_GetImage, File_Replace, File_GetToken, File_GetAllTokens

2. Section 8 (Serial Port Functions) - Lines 14297-15603: SerialPort Class, com_open, com_close, com_read (9 syntaxes), com_read_string (9 syntaxes), com_write (3 syntaxes), com_writeline (3 syntaxes)

3. Section 9 (Socket Functions) - Lines 15603-16923: Socket Class, socket_open, socket_close, socket_read (9 syntaxes), socket_read_string (9 syntaxes), socket_send (3 syntaxes), socket_sendline (3 syntaxes)

4. Section 10 (Manual Decision Functions) - Lines 16923-17299: MDecision Class with Reset(), Title(), Description(), Timeout(), TimeoutDefaultCase(), Case(), Show()

5. Section 11 (Parameterized Objects) - Lines 17299-18112: Point, Base, TCP, VPoint, IO, Robot, FT

6. Section 12 (Robot Teach Class) - Lines 18112-18990: TPoint (4 constructs), TBase (2 constructs + GetValue, SetValue, ConvShift), TTCP (3 constructs)

7. Section 13 (Robot Motion & Vision Job Functions) - Lines 18990-21803: QueueTag, WaitQueueTag, CheckQueueTag, StopAndClearBuffer, PTP (4 syntaxes), Line (2 syntaxes), Circle (2 syntaxes), PLine (2 syntaxes), Move_PTP (2 syntaxes), Move_Line (2 syntaxes), Move_PLine (2 syntaxes), ChangeBase (4 syntaxes), ChangeTCP (7 syntaxes), ChangeLoad, CollisionCheck (2 syntaxes), PVTEnter, PVTExit, PVTPoint (4 syntaxes), PVTPause, PVTResume, PathOffset_Set (7 syntaxes), PathOffset_Get (2 syntaxes), PathOffset_IsEnabled, PathOffset_AlphaFilter, PathOffset_MaxOffset, Vision_DoJob, Vision_DoJob_PTP (2 syntaxes), Vision_DoJob_Line (3 syntaxes)

8. Section 14 (Vision Functions) - Lines 22012-22330: Vision_IsJobAvailable, Vision_GetOutputArraySize, Vision_GetOutputArrayValue, Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputCount, Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue

9. Section 15 (External Script) - Lines 22330-23352: Listen Node description, ScriptListen (3 syntaxes), ScriptExit, Communication Protocol, TMSCT format, TMSTA (SubCmd 00, 01, 90-99), CPERR, Priority Commands

10. Section 16 (Modbus Functions) - Lines 23352-23992: ModbusTCP Class (Preset, IODDPreset), ModbusRTU Class (Preset, IODDPreset), modbus_open, modbus_close, modbus_read (syntax 1 partial)

The session ended at approximately line 23992, in the middle of Section 16 (modbus_read), with remaining sections:
- modbus_read (remaining syntaxes)
- modbus_write
- Section 17: TM Ethernet Slave (svr_read, svr_write, TMSVR protocol)
- Section 18: Profinet Functions
- Section 19: EtherNet/IP Functions
- Section 20: Compliance Functions
- Section 21: TouchStop Functions
- Section 22: Force Control Functions

Then the final task of compiling all information into a complete reference document.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   사용자가 TMscript 프로그래밍 언어 매뉴얼 PDF를 처음부터 끝까지 빠짐없이 읽고 완전한 레퍼런스 자료를 수집하도록 요청. 다음 10가지 항목을 추출해야 함:
   - 데이터 타입 (int, float, double, string, byte[], bool 등), 배열, 변환 규칙
   - 변수 선언, 스코프, 시스템 변수 목록, 전역/로컬 차이
   - 연산자 전체 (산술, 비교, 논리, 비트, 대입 등)
   - 제어문 (if/else, for, while, switch, break, continue, return 등)
   - 모든 내장 함수 목록 (카테고리별, 시그니처/파라미터 타입/반환값/설명 포함)
   - 모션 명령 (PTP, Line, Circle, Move 관련)
   - 통신 관련 (소켓, Modbus, Listen Node)
   - Listen Node 명령어 포맷
   - 예제 코드 전체
   - External Script 관련 내용
   파일: `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\Programming-Language-TMscript_2.18_Rev1.0_EN.pdf`

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - TMscript: Techman Robot Inc.의 프로그래밍 언어 (소프트웨어 버전 2.18, 문서 버전 1.0, 2024-12-17)
   - Flow 프로젝트 및 Script 프로젝트 양쪽에서 사용
   - 데이터 타입: byte(8bit unsigned), int(32bit signed), float(32bit IEEE-754), double(64bit IEEE-754), bool, string, 각 타입의 배열
   - 엔디언: 기본 Little Endian (string은 UTF-8)
   - 스코프: define(전역), main/closestop/errorstop/커스텀 함수(로컬)
   - 스레드: ThreadRun, ThreadID, ThreadState, ThreadExit (메인 스레드만 모션 함수 호출 가능)
   - 파일 함수: TextFiles/XmlFiles/USB/네트워크 경로 지원, 최대 10MB
   - 시리얼 포트: SerialPort 클래스, com_read/write (prefix/suffix 기반 파싱 지원)
   - 소켓: Socket 클래스, TCP/IP 클라이언트, socket_read/send (prefix/suffix 기반 파싱 지원)
   - Modbus: ModbusTCP/ModbusRTU 클래스, Preset/IODDPreset 메서드
   - Listen Node: TCP 서버(포트 5890), TMSCT/TMSTA/CPERR 프로토콜
   - 로봇 모션: PTP, Line, Circle, PLine, Move_PTP, Move_Line, Move_PLine (버퍼 큐 방식)
   - 파라미터화 객체: Point, Base, TCP, VPoint, IO, Robot, FT
   - 티치 클래스: TPoint, TBase, TTCP
   - PathOffset: 경로 오프셋 보정 (AlphaFilter 적용)
   - PVT 모드: Position-Velocity-Time 기반 모션 제어
   - CollisionCheck: 사전 충돌 검사 함수

   // TPoint: 변수명이 포인트명
   TPoint P1 = {0,-282.75,1094.9,90,0,0}           // Construct 1: 좌표
   TPoint P2 = 517.5,-147.8,442.45,180,0,90
   TPoint P3 = "D",{522.07,130.75,442.45,180,0,120}  // Construct 2: 타입+좌표
   // 타입: "C"=좌표(저장X), "D"=좌표(저장O), "J"=관절(저장X), "JD"=관절(저장O)
   TPoint P7 = "D",{...},"RobotBase","RobotEndFlange" // Construct 3: 타입+좌표+베이스+TCP
   TPoint tp1 = "C",{...},{0,0,0,0,0,0},"base1","tcp1" // Construct 4: 타입+좌표+관절+베이스+TCP
   
   // TPoint 멤버: Value, Joint, Pose, Flange, BaseName, TCPName
   //              TeachValue, TeachJoint, TeachPose
   
   // TBase: 변수명이 베이스명
   TBase base1 = {0,0,90,0,0,0}                    // Construct 1
   TBase base6 = "D",0,90,90,0,0,0                 // Construct 2: 타입+값
   // 타입: "C"=저장X, "D"=저장O, "V"=비전(vision_로 시작해야)
   // RobotBase는 선언만 가능, 값 무시
   
   // TBase 멤버: Value(R/W), Type(R), TeachValue(R)

   base1.GetValue(int) → float[]   // 복수 베이스에서 인덱스로 가져오기
   base1.SetValue(int, float[]) → bool
   base1.ConvShift(float[]) → float[]  // 시프트 적용한 새 베이스 계산
   base1.ConvShift(float,float,float,float,float,float) → float[]
   base1.ConvShift(float[], float[]) → float[]  // 베이스값+시프트
   
   // TTCP: 변수명이 TCP명

   TTCP tcp2 = {0,0,90,0,0,0},2                    // 질량 포함
   TTCP tcp5 = "C",{0,0,90,0,0,0}                  // Construct 2: 타입+값
   TTCP tcp6 = "D",{0,90,90,0,0,0},2               // 타입+값+질량
   // visionTCP_xxx 명명 규칙 (타입 "V")

 25120→ 17. TM Ethernet Slave
 25121→
 25122→      Ethernet Slave comes with functions established with Socket TCP based on the framework of
 25123→client/server connections. Once enabled, the robot establishes a Socket TCP Listener Serve to send
 25124→the robot status and data to all of the connected clients or receive the contents from the clients to
 25125→execute the respective instructions and update the respective information periodically and promptly
 25126→without the real-time guarantee.
 25127→
 25128→Like the Modbus Slave, the Ethernet Slave will automatically start on its own after power cycling
 25129→
 25130→if it was previously set to Enable. The established IP and Port will be shown in the notice window.
 25131→
 25132→IP  TMflow  System  Network  IP Address
 25133→
 25134→Port 5891
 25135→
 25136→17.1 GUI Setting
 25137→
 25138→Enable/Disable      Enable or disable Ethernet Slave
 25139→IP Filter           IP whitelist
 25140→                    Sets ranges for eligible IP addresses that are allowed to connect to the Ethernet
 25141→Write               Slave. If no filters are set, all devices on the network can connect to the Ethernet
 25142→Permission          Slave.
 25143→                    If checked, allows devices within the corresponding IP range to write to the
 25144→                    Ethernet Server with TMSVR commands.
 25145→
 25146→For example, setting IP Filter.
 25147→      Group 1 192.168.1.100 ~ 200 denotes IP 192.168.1.100, 192.168.1.101, ... , and
 25148→      192.168.1.200 are available for connections.
 25149→      Group 2 192.168.2.100 ~ 200 denotes IP 192.168.2.100, 192.168.2.101, ... , and
 25150→      192.168.2.200 are available for connections.
 25151→      If the IP address of the client is not in the range of the IPs listed above, it rejects the client
 25152→      to connect.
 25153→
 25154→    Group 1, 192.168.1.100 ~ 200, has permission to write, so clients connecting within this
 25155→    group range sending data to Ethernet Slave makes Ethernet Slave write data. Group 2,
 25156→    192.168.2.100 ~ 200, does not have permission to write. When sending data to Ethernet
 25157→    Slave, it does not write data and will respond with the error code of write permission.
 25158→

 25280→ 17.4 Data Table
 25281→
 25282→Users can use the items listed the Data Table to customize the required data content as well
 25283→
 25284→as configure the communication protocol to transmit between the Ethernet Slave and clients,
 25285→
 25286→and save the settings as a communication file. When the Ethernet Slave is enabled, the data
 25287→
 25288→items in the communication file will be established with the relevant data content to the item to
 25289→
 25290→send to the connected clients periodically (no real-time guarantee). The types of the data
 25291→
 25292→format is defined by the settings in the communication file. The client can send data to the
 25293→
 25294→server with any type of the supported data formats.
 25295→
 25296→In the protocol, the types of the supported data format are:
 25297→
 25298→BINARY Binary format, converse in Byte array (Little Endian / UTF8)
 25299→
 25300→STRING              String format, similar to the external command format
 25301→
 25302→JSON                JSON string format
 25303→
 25304→The configuration interface is a left-to-right mechanism. Users can add items at the left to the
 25305→communication data table at the right and adjust the arrangement order of each item in the
 25306→communication data table at the right. In the content to send, there will always be an
 25307→item, Robot_Link, predefined in Ethernet Slave as a type of byte with the attribute of read-only to
 25308→denote whether to connect to the robot.
 25309→
 25310→1. Predefined
 25311→      Items and settings in this section are defined by TMflow, and the data content of the items is
 25312→      updated by TMflow. The defined items are the general statuses of the robot, such as the
 25313→      coordinates of the robot, the state of the project, the state of the electrical control box, or the
 25314→      IO related statuses, such as digital input / digital output, analog input / analog output.
 25315→
 25316→2. User defined
 25317→      Items and settings in this section are defined by TMflow users for project programs to read /
 25318→      write item data through the Expression Editor or for external users to read / write item data
 25319→      through the TMSVR commands over a TCP/IP connection. With the user defined tab, the
 25320→      project programs can work with external communication devices as a data exchange protocol.
 25321→      The item list in the user-defined tab can be saved as a custom-defined fil to be edited or
 25322→      exchanged data in the future.
 25323→
 25324→3. Global Variable
 25325→      In the global variable tab, the variable list created by the TMflow users provides a way to directly
 25326→      use the variable name for read / write operations in the project programming, and the external
 25327→      communication devices can read / write global variables with the communication protocol.
 25328→
 25329→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                        Document Version: 1.0  408
 25330→

 25332→

 25334→ 17.5 Communication Protocol
 25335→
 25336→                                      Length
 25337→
 25338→Start Byte    Hdr          Len        Data                                        Checksum End Byte1 End Byte2
 25339→     $      Header
 25340→                    ,      Length  ,  Data                                  ,  *  Checksum            \r  \n
 25341→
 25342→                           Checksum (XOR of these Bytes)
 25343→
 25344→Name          Size ASCII HEX Description
 25345→
 25346→Start Byte 1           $   0x24 Start Byte for Communication
 25347→
 25348→Header        X                       Header for Communication
 25349→
 25350→Separator 1            ,   0x2C Separator between Header and Length
 25351→
 25352→Length        Y                       Length of Data
 25353→
 25354→Separator 1            ,   0x2C Separator between Length and Data
 25355→
 25356→Data          Z                       Communication Data
 25357→
 25358→Separator 1            ,   0x2C Separator between Data and Checksum
 25359→
 25360→Sign          1        *   0x2A Begin Sign of Checksum
 25361→
 25362→Checksum 2                            Checksum of Communication
 25363→
 25364→End Byte 1 1           \r  0x0D
 25365→
 25366→End Byte 2 1           \n  0x0A End Byte of Communication
 25367→
 25368→*Using the same communication protocol with external commands.
 25369→
 25370→1. Header
 25371→
 25372→Defines the purpose of communication packets. Different headers come with different
 25373→definitions of communication packets and data.
 25374→
 25375→ TMSVR Defines the function of TM Ethernet Slave
 25376→ CPERR Defines the errors of the communication packets such as packer errors, checksum
 25377→
 25378→                   errors, header errors, and so on.
 25379→                      *Using the same content definitions with CPERR in external commands.
 25380→
 25381→2. Length
 25382→
 25383→The length indicates the length in the UTF8 bytes occupied by Data. Users can use decimal,
 25384→
 25385→hexadecimal, or binary format. The maximum length is 32 bits.
 25386→
 25387→For example,
 25388→
 25389→         $TMSVR,100,Data,*CS\r\n // 100 in decimal indicates the data length is 100 bytes
 25390→
 25391→         $TMSVR,0x100,Data,*CS\r\n // 0x100 in hexadecimal indicates the data length is 256 bytes
 25392→
 25393→         $TMSVR,0b100,Data,*CS\r\n // 0b100 in binary indicates the data length is 4 bytes
 25394→
 25395→         $TMSVR,8,1, ,*CS\r\n         // indicates the length of Data, 1, , is 8 bytes (UTF8)
 25396→
 25397→3. Data
 25398→
 25399→The content of the communication packet can support any character (including 0x00 .. 0xFF and
 25400→uses UTF8 encoding), and the data length determines by Length. The purpose and description
 25401→defined in Data must be defined by the header.
 25402→

 25408→ 4. Checksum
 25409→
 25410→    The checksum of the communication packet. The calculation method is XOR (exclusive OR).
 25411→    The calculation range is all Bytes between $ and * (excluding $ and *) as shown below.
 25412→
 25413→                  $TMSVR,100,Data,*CS\r\n
 25414→    Checksum = Byte[1] ^ Byte[2] ... ^ Byte[N-6]
 25415→    The checksum format is set to 2 bytes in hexadecimal (but not 0x), such as
 25416→
 25417→                  $TMSVR,5,10,OK,*7E
 25418→    CS = 0x54 ^ 0x4D ^ 0x53 ^ 0x56 ^ 0x52 ^ 0x2C ^ 0x35 ^ 0x2C ^ 0x31 ^ 0x30 ^ 0x2C ^ 0x4F ^
 25419→    0x4B ^ 0x2C = 0x7E
 25420→    CS = 7E (0x37 0x45)
 25421→
 25422→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                        Document Version: 1.0  410
 25423→
 25424→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 25425→
 25426→5F., No. 58-2, Huaya 2nd Rd., Guishan Dist., Taoyuan City, 333411 , Taiwan
 25427→ 17.6 TMSVR
 25428→
 25429→Start Byte  Hdr                     Len              Data                            Checksum End Byte1 End Byte2
 25430→
 25431→$           TMSVR   ,   Length              ,        Data                      ,  *  Checksum            \r  \n
 25432→
 25433→                    ID                         Mode                                   Content
 25434→                                                                                  Item and Value
 25435→                    Transaction ID       ,     0/1/2/3/                     ,
 25436→                                               11/12/13
 25437→
 25438→TMSVR is defined as the TM Ethernet Slave protocol. The Data section of the packet is further
 25439→
 25440→divided into three segments, ID (Transaction ID), Mode (Content Mode), and Content (Item and
 25441→
 25442→Value), separated with commas and described below.
 25443→
 25444→   ID               The transaction number expressed in any alphanumeric characters. (Reports the
 25445→
 25446→                    CPERR 04 error if a non-alphanumeric byte is encountered.) When used as a
 25447→
 25448→                    communication packet response, it is a transaction number that identifies which
 25449→
 25450→                    group of commands to respond.
 25451→
 25452→   ,                the symbol to separate
 25453→
 25454→   Mode The mode as the format of the data content
 25455→
 25456→                    Indicates the server responds to the client command in string format.
 25457→
 25458→                    Indicates the content data type in binary format
 25459→
 25460→                    Indicates the content data type in string format
 25461→
 25462→                    Indicates the content data type in JSON format
 25463→
 25464→                    11 Indicates the content data type in binary format (Request read)
 25465→
 25466→                    12 Indicates the content data type in string format (Request read)
 25467→
 25468→                    13 Indicates the content data type in JSON format (Request read)
 25469→
 25470→                     1/2/3 are for client write to server and client read from server. The client read
 25471→
 25472→                    from server is that the server sends contents to the connected client
 25473→
 25474→                    periodically.
 25475→
 25476→                     11/12/13 are for client read from server with request read, which is the client
 25477→
 25478→                    sends read request for the item and the server responds with the item value
 25479→
 25480→                    to the client.
 25481→
 25482→   ,                the symbol to separate
 25483→
 25484→   Content The data content. Formatted by the mode definition.
 25485→
 25486→Note
 25487→      TMSVR command is for the client and the server to communicate in both directions. Under
 25488→
 25489→normal circumstances, the server will broadcast the data items from the Transmit and User
 25490→Defined communication files to the connected clients periodically. When the server sends to
 25491→the client, the data is sent to the client to read from the server with no response to the server

25491→the client, the data is sent to the client to read from the server with no response to the server
 25492→required. When the client sends to the server, the data is received by the server from the client
 25493→to write with response to the client required.
 25494→
 25495→      The transaction number, when the server sends data, cycles from 0 to 9 with each iteration.
 25496→When the client sends data to the server, the transaction number can be in any alphanumeric
 25497→characters customized at the client side. If the communication packet format is checked and
 25498→correct , the server will reply the client with the command processing status by the transaction
 25499→number in the packet.
 25500→
 25501→      When the client sends data to the server, the server checks whether all the criteria to write
 25502→are correct before performing data writing. If there is any error with the write command, no data
 25503→
 25504→Programming Language TMscript Software Version: 2.18                              Document Version: 1.0      411
 25505→
 25506→TECHMAN ROBOT INC.
 25507→
 25508→5F., No. 58-2, Huaya 2nd Rd., Guishan Dist., Taoyuan City, 333411 , Taiwan
 25509→ will be overwritten. The criteria to write for inspection are:
 25510→
 25511→1. The validity of the mode as the format of the data content
 25512→2. The connected client's write permissions based on the IP Filter.
 25513→3. The data content matches to the mode.
 25514→4. The item to write exists in the Transmit or User Defined communication file.
 25515→5. The attribute of the item to write is not read only.
 25516→6. The robot is in the appropriate mode (M/A).
 25517→7. The written data matches the data type of each item.
 25518→
 25519→      When the Mode is 11/12/13, the request read method is used. The client sends an item
 25520→request and the server responds to the item value. The requested items can be the items in the
 25521→Predefined area without limited to check the periodical delivery, but in the User defined area
 25522→and the Global Variable area, for custom definitions, it still need to check the periodical delivery
 25523→to get the request.
 25524→
 25525→      When the client sends an item request, it is not limited to only one item but multiple items
 25526→can be acquired at once. When the item request is success, the server will send the item value
 25527→to the client based on the format matching to Mode 11/12/13. However, if the item request is
 25528→failed such as the item name does not exist, the server will send the command processing
 25529→status based on the Mode 0 format.
 25530→
 25531→0. Mode = 0 (the status the server responds to the client command
 25532→     processing)
 25533→
 25534→After the server receives and processes a write command from a client, it will respond with
 25535→
 25536→another TMSVR command with Mode 0. The details for Mode 0 are as follows.
 25537→
 25538→Data
 25539→
 25540→      ID                 Mode    Error Code                                    Error Description
 25541→
 25542→Transaction , 0 ,                00 .. 07                                   ,
 25543→
 25544→      ID
 25545→
 25546→Transaction ID           Defined while the client sends the command for the server to
 25547→
 25548→respond with.
 25549→
 25550→Mode                0 for the server to respond to the client
 25551→
 25552→Error Code Error code definitions. Fixed as 2 bytes and in hexadecimal (but not 0x)
 25553→
 25554→                    00   Correct writing. No error.
 25555→
 25556→                    01   The communication format or mode is not supported. (Ex. Mode =
 25557→
 25558→                    99)
 25559→
 25560→                    02   The connected client is not permitted to write. (IP filer without write
 25561→
 25562→                    permission)
 25563→
 25564→                    03   The communication format and the data content format are
 25565→
 25566→                    mismatched.
 25567→
 25568→                         (Ex. Mode = 3, but the data content is not in JSON format)
 25569→
 25570→                    04   Item to write or read does not exist.
 25571→
 25572→                    05   Unable to write to read-only items.
 25573→
 25574→                    06   Incorrect M/A mode while writing.
 25575→
 25576→                    07   Values to write mismatches with the configured type or the size.
 25577→
 25578→Error Description Error description, following the error code.

 25659→ 1. Mode = 1 BINARY
 25660→
 25661→The data content is transmitted in binary mode by converting the data item name with the Little
 25662→Endian value and the value with UTF8 to a byte array accordingly. The format is shown as below.
 25663→
 25664→Data
 25665→
 25666→       ID                       Mode                                            Content
 25667→                                                                            Item and Value
 25668→Transaction ,                   1                       ,
 25669→
 25670→       ID
 25671→
 25672→                    Length of Item                         Item             Length of Value        Value                    ...
 25673→                                                                              2 bytes Little  Little Endian /
 25674→                    2 bytes Little Endian UTF8                                    Endian
 25675→                                                                                                   UTF8
 25676→
 25677→Length of Item 2 bytes in Little Endian, value from 0 to 65535 indicating the length of the
 25678→
 25679→item that follows
 25680→
 25681→Item                item name
 25682→
 25683→Length of Value 2 bytes in Little Endian), value from 0 to 65535 indicating the length of
 25684→
 25685→the data that follows
 25686→
 25687→Value               data value
 25688→
 25689→Suppose taking Check TCP_Value float[] and Ctrl_DO0 byte as the communication data and
 25690→transmitting in binary mode.
 25691→
 25692→> 24 54 4D 53 56 52 2C // $TMSVR,                                           // Header
 25693→
 25694→       36 39 2C                       // 69,                                // Length
 25695→
 25696→       30 2C 31 2C                            // 0,1,                                  // transaction ID 0, mode 1, binary
 25697→
 25698→       0A 00 52 6F 62 6F 74 5F 4C 69 6E 6B 01 00 00 // Robot_Link=0
 25699→
 25700→       // The name occupied 10 bytes, the value, 1 byte
 25701→
 25702→       09 00 54 43 50 5F 56 61 6C 75 65 18 00 00 00 80 3F 00 00 80 3F 00 00 80 3F CD
 25703→
 25704→       CC CC 3D CD CC 4C 3E CD CC CC 3D                                                // TCP_Value={1,1,1,0.1,0.2,0.1}
 25705→
 25706→       // The name occupied 9 bytes, the value, 24 bytes                    // Ctrl_DO0=0
 25707→
 25708→       08 00 43 74 72 6C 5F 44 4F 30 01 00 00
 25709→
 25710→       // The name occupied 8 bytes, the value, 1 byte
 25711→
 25712→       2C 2A 39 36 0D 0A              // ,*96\r\n                           // Checksum
 25713→
 25714→< 24 54 4D 53 56 52 2C // $TMSVR, // Header
 25715→
 25716→       31 38 2C                       // 18,                                // Length
 25717→
 25718→       54 31 2C 31 2C                 // T1,1,                              // transaction ID T1, mode 1, binary
 25719→
 25720→       08 00 43 74 72 6C 5F 44 4F 30 01 00 01 // Ctrl_DO0=1
 25721→
 25722→       // The name occupied 8 bytes, the value, 1 byte
 25723→
 25724→       2C 2A 37 41 0D 0A              // ,*7A\r\n                           // Checksum
 25725→
 25726→> $TMSVR,10,T1,0,00,OK,*1E\r\n

 26006→ 12. Mode = 12 STRING (Request read)
 26007→
 26008→The data content is transmitted as a string with the name and value of the data item in the Script
 26009→string of an external command. The format is shown as below.
 26010→
 26011→Data (client to server)
 26012→
 26013→      ID                       Mode                                             Content
 26014→                                                                            Item and Value
 26015→Transaction ,                  12    ,
 26016→
 26017→      ID
 26018→
 26019→                         Item           \r\n                                ...  No Value
 26020→                                                                                 required.
 26021→
 26022→Item                Item name
 26023→
 26024→\r\n                The newline characters. Required only as a delimiter if the next item comes.
 26025→
 26026→Suppose taking TCP_Value float[] and Ctrl_DO0 byte, Ctrl_DO1 byte, g_ss string[] as the
 26027→communication data and transmitting in STRING mode.
 26028→
 26029→server periodical delivery                            // Robot_Link=0            // transaction ID 9, mode 2
 26030→> $TMSVR,97,9,2,Robot_Link=0\r\n
 26031→                                                      // TCP_Value={1,1,1,0.1,0.2,0.1}
 26032→      TCP_Value={1,1,1,0.1,0.2,0.1}\r\n
 26033→      Ctrl_DO0=1\r\n                                  // Ctrl_DO0=1
 26034→      Ctrl_DO1=0\r\n
 26035→      g_ss={"Hi","TM","Robot"},*77\r\n                // Ctrl_DO1=0
 26036→
 26037→                                                      // g_ss={"Hi","TM","Robot"}
 26038→
 26039→client requested to read
 26040→
 26041→< $TMSVR,28,Q2,12,Robot_Link\r\n // Item Robot_Link
 26042→
 26043→                                     // transaction ID Q2, mode 12 JSON (Request read)
 26044→
 26045→      TCP_Mass,*0E\r\n                                // Item TCP_Mass
 26046→
 26047→server replied with the item value
 26048→
 26049→> $TMSVR,30,Q2,12,Robot_Link=0\r\n // server responds to ID Q2, mode 12
 26050→
 26051→      TCP_Mass=0,*09\r\n
 26052→
 26053→* server replied the same content format as Mode = 2 STRING

 26121→ 18. Profinet Functions
 26122→
 26123→      The robot communicates with external controllers via the Profinet communication protocol. In
 26124→the mechanism of the Profinet communication protocol, the robot works as a Profinet IO device for
 26125→external devices to read and write the robot data. Meanwhile, TMflow monitors the table of data
 26126→receiving from external devices and the table of data sending to external devices with Profinet
 26127→functions as well as changes the custom definition section in the table of data sending to external
 26128→devices.
 26129→
 26130→Communication Data Table
 26131→      The data table is composed of the input data and the output data. Input Data Table is for
 26132→      external devices posting on the robot, and Output Data Table is for the robot sending to
 26133→      external devices. Both of the data tables come with System Definition Section and
 26134→      Custom Definition Section for data.
 26135→
 26136→1. System Definition Section Items and settings are defined by the robot, and the data
 26137→      contents are updated by the robot or external devices. The defined items are robot status
 26138→      relevant such as robot bases, project status, control box status, or input/output status
 26139→      relevant such as digital I/Os and analog I/Os. Users can use Profinet functions to read
 26140→      the input data table and the output data table in the system definition section.
 26141→
 26142→2. Custom Definition Section Items and settings are defined by users, and the data
 26143→      contents are updated by users or external devices. In the meantime of the project editing,
 26144→      users can use Profinet functions to read and write the output data table in the custom
 26145→      definition section or read input data table in the custom definition section as well as use
 26146→      the custom definition section as a data exchange register between the project and
 26147→      external devices.
 26148→
 26149→Communication       Data        TMflow Profinet                             External Device
 26150→Data Table          Section
 26151→(at the robot's                 Function Permissions Permissions
 26152→viewpoint)          System
 26153→                    Definition  Read                                        Write
 26154→Input Data          Section
 26155→Table               Custom      Read                                        Write
 26156→                    Definition
 26157→Output Data         Section     Read                                        Read
 26158→Table               System
 26159→                    Definition
 26160→                    Section     Read/Write                                  Read
 26161→                    Custom
 26162→                    Definition
 26163→                    Section

 28802→ 19. EtherNet/IP Functions
 28803→
 28804→      The robot communicates with external controllers via the EtherNet/IP communication
 28805→protocol. In the mechanism of the EtherNet/IP communication protocol, the robot works as a
 28806→EtherNet/IP IO device for external devices to read and write the robot data. Meanwhile, TMflow
 28807→monitors the table of data receiving from external devices and the table of data sending to
 28808→external devices with EtherNet/IP functions as well as changes the custom definition section in the
 28809→table of data sending to external devices.
 28810→
 28811→      Communication Data Table
 28812→            The data table is composed of the input data and the output data. Input Data Table is for
 28813→            external devices posting on the robot, and Output Data Table is for the robot sending to
 28814→            external devices. Both of the data tables come with System Definition Section and
 28815→            Custom Definition Section for data.
 28816→
 28817→      1. System Definition Section Items and settings are defined by the robot, and the data
 28818→            contents are updated by the robot or external devices. The defined items are robot status
 28819→            relevant such as robot bases, project status, control box status, or input/output status
 28820→            relevant such as digital I/Os and analog I/Os. Users can use EtherNet/IP functions to
 28821→            read the input data table and the output data table in the system definition section.
 28822→
 28823→      2. Custom Definition Section Items and settings are defined by users, and the data
 28824→            contents are updated by users or external devices. In the meantime of the project editing,
 28825→            users can use EtherNet/IP functions to read and write the output data table in the custom
 28826→            definition section or read input data table in the custom definition section as well as use
 28827→            the custom definition section as a data exchange register between the project and
 28828→            external devices.
 28829→
 28830→Communication       Data        TMflow EtherNet/IP External Device
 28831→Data Table          Section     Function Permissions Permissions
 28832→(at the robot's
 28833→viewpoint)          System      Read                                        Write
 28834→                    Definition

 28836→Table               Custom
 28837→                    Definition  Read                                        Read
 28838→Output Data         Section
 28839→Table               System
 28840→                    Definition

 28842→                    Custom

 32066→control motion, exits the function, and returns the result values.
 32067→
 32068→(3) Advanced
 32069→
 32070→PTP("JPP",{0,0,90,0,90,0},50,200,0,false)
 32071→
 32072→cp1.AdvSet("X", true, 100, -100, 30, 30) // Set direction X with advanced parameters
 32073→cp1.AdvSet("Z", true, 100, -100, 30, 30) // Set direction Z with advanced parameters
 32074→
 32075→cp1.AdvSet("X", true, 100, -100, 40, 40) // Set direction X with advanced parameters (will overwrite
 32076→
 32077→                                                      the previous AdvSet X parameter)
 32078→
 32079→int re = cp1.Start()                                  // Start the compliance control motion
 32080→
 32081→// Set direction X and Z for compliance control. Once the moving distance is comprehended, it stop the
 32082→
 32083→control motion, exits the function, and returns the result values.
 32084→
 32085→(4) Impedance
 32086→
 32087→PTP("JPP",{0,0,90,0,90,0},50,200,0,false)
 32088→
 32089→cp1.Impedance("all", 1)                               // Release all axes
 32090→
 32091→cp1.DInput("ControlBox", 0, "H")                      // When ControlBox DI0 is High
 32092→
 32093→int re = cp1.Start()                                  // Start the compliance control motion
 32094→
 32095→// Release all axes. The robot stays put in the current position and complies with external forces to
 32096→
 32097→lessen the torque on the joints. When the stop criterion, ControlBox DI0 is High, is comprehended, it
 32098→
 32099→stop the control motion, exits the function, and returns the result values.

 33479→actively until proceeding to read or write.

   // Mode 0: 서버→클라이언트 응답 (에러코드 00~07)
   //   00=OK, 01=NotSupport, 02=WritePermission, 03=InvalidData,
   //   04=NotExist, 05=ReadOnly, 06=ModeError, 07=ValueError
   // Mode 1: BINARY (Little Endian / UTF8 바이트 배열, 2바이트 아이템명 길이+아이템명+2바이트값길이+값)

   // Mode 11/12/13: Request Read (클라이언트→서버 요청 읽기, 값 없이 아이템명만)
   //   Mode 11: Binary 방식, Mode 12: String 방식, Mode 13: JSON 방식
